architecture.summary: Sliceformer reformulates static program slicing as a sequence-to-sequence task using CodeT5+ (770M parameters). The system consists of three stages: (1) dataflow-aware pretraining on CodeSearchNet, which teaches the model to recognize data dependencies through statement permutation constrained by data-flow graphs and dataflow-guided span corruption at variable and statement granularities; (2) supervised fine-tuning on labeled slicing data from CodeNet-Slice with special control markers; (3) constrained beam search decoding that applies lexical constraints (restricting output vocabulary to input tokens) and syntactic constraints (using Tree Similarity Edit Distance monotonicity to filter structurally invalid candidates at statement boundaries). Information flows from source code + slicing criterion (input tokens) through the encoder-decoder model, then through constrained decoding that reads the original code vocabulary and computes AST edit distances to validate slice structure.
trained_components: CodeT5+ encoder-decoder with dataflow-aware pretraining objectives

biblio.title: Static Program Slicing Using Language Models With Dataflow-Aware Pretraining and Constrained Decoding

claims.statement: Sliceformer achieves ExactMatch improvements of 6.4% on Java and 21.9% on Python compared to the strongest baseline (NS-slicer with GraphCodeBERT)
On Java programs, Sliceformer attains ExactMatch of 92.20% versus NS-slicer (GraphCodeBERT) at 85.77%
On Python programs, Sliceformer reaches ExactMatch of 83.15% compared to NS-slicer (GraphCodeBERT) at 61.25%

baseline.name: CodeT5+ (SFT without dataflow-aware pretraining and constrained decoding)

claims.statement: Sliceformer outperforms CodeT5+ (SFT) by 5.0% on Java and 5.9% on Python in ExactMatch
LLM-based methods (GPT-5 with CoT) achieve only 14.00% and 13.00% ExactMatch on Java and Python respectively
Sliceformer achieves average inference latency of 0.296 seconds per task on Java dataset
Dataflow-aware pretraining is designed for encoder-decoder architectures and is not directly applicable to decoder-only models, though constrained decoding is architecture-agnostic
Sliceformer is evaluated on only two programming languages (Java and Python); additional engineering and empirical validation are required to extend the method to other languages
